items.title: Bukayo Saka signs new Arsenal contract to 2030
items.summary: Bukayo Saka has signed a new contract to stay at Arsenal, with sources telling ESPN it is a five-year deal.

items.title: Benfica: 'Smear campaign' after Viní allegations
items.summary: SL Benfica have condemned the "smear campaign" to which their player Gianluca Prestianni has been subjected to following allegations that he racially abused Real Madrid's Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Ange regrets Forest job, 'too soon' after Spurs
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has admitted he regrets taking the head coach role at Nottingham Forest this season, believing it was "too soon" after he left Tottenham Hotspur.

items.title: Gordon makes UCL history with 4 goals in 1st half
items.summary: Newcastle United forward Anthony Gordon has become just the second player to score four goals in the first half of a UEFA Champions League game and the first to do so in the knockout stage.

items.title: Are Arsenal in danger of repeating history?
items.summary: Arsenal's disappointing 2-2 draw at Wolves on Wednesday has similarities to what happened to the Gunners 18 years ago.

items.title: Arsenal geeft het helemaal weg: 'Het is te voorgeprogrammeerd'
items.summary: Arsenal verspeelde woensdag in de absolute slotfase dure punten tegen hekkensluiter Wolverhampton Wanderers en ziet Manchester City langzaam dichterbij komen. In deze video leggen Pieter Zwart en Bas van den Hoven uit waar het aan schort bij de ploeg van Mikel Arteta.

items.title: Contractnieuws in Noord-Londen: Saka verlengt bij Arsenal
items.summary: Arsenal heeft het contract van Bukayo Saka (24) opengebroken en verlengd tot medio 2031. Zo verzekert de koploper van de Premier League zich ook voor de komende jaren van de diensten van haar sterspeler.

items.title: Does the Premier League table get you down? Try one of our five alternatives
items.summary: What if games ended at 90 minutes, set-pieces goals were scrapped, or shots that hit the woodwork counted? By Opta Analyst A league table is a pretty helpful barometer of each team’s fortunes. That’s why we use it to decide where teams finish at the end of the season. However, league tables do not tell us the whole story. Arsenal are top in reality (just about), but they might not be in all of our alternative Premier League tables. Continue reading...

items.title: Rosenior: No place in football for anyone found guilty of racism
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior said anyone in football who is found guilty of racism “should not be in the game.”

items.title: Dit kan AZ verwachten van FC Noah
items.summary: In de tussenronde van de Conference League treft AZ een grote onbekende. Geen toeval, want FC Noah bestaat pas een decennium als profclub. De huidige nummer vijf van de Armeense competitie heeft voor een flinke stunt gezorgd, door Europees te overleven. Analyse van een opponent die een klassieke vreemdelingenlegioen-tactiek hanteert.

items.title: AZ Quiz: een magische Conference League-campagne
items.summary: AZ hervat donderdagavond zijn Europese campagne in de tussenronde van de Conference League, de club zal hopen op zo'n mooi seizoen als in 2022/23. Toen werd er via drie voorrondes het hoofdtoernooi bereikt en schopte AZ het uiteindelijk tot de halve finale. Voor deze quiz richten we onze blik op de AZ-selectie van toen.

items.title: Is it happening again? Arsenal's stumble in title race follows familiar pattern
items.summary: Arsenal's Premier League championship charge suffered yet another wobble on Wednesday, leaving many Gunners fans to wonder: "Is it happening again?"

items.title: Stewart to join England set-up? 'There's no job to say yes to'
items.summary: Alec Stewart is not banking on a role in England's post-Ashes rebuild, insisting "there isn't a job to say yes or no to".

items.title: Clubs frustrated over wait for Fifa to share £185m of Club World Cup money
items.summary: Clubs who did not play are to get solidarity payments No formula determined for dividing the money Frustration is growing among clubs globally at the extended wait for £185m of solidarity payments promised by Fifa on the back of last summer’s Club World Cup. Clubs that did not participate in the tournament were promised a share of the sum, designed to ensure a proportion of the event’s funding was distributed throughout the football pyramid. If shared equally it would amount to about £50,000 for every top-flight club in the world but, more than seven months after the Club World Cup’s conclusion, there is no sign of the money and no timescale for its distribution. The Guardian understands Fifa is yet to determine how the money will be allocated. Continue reading...

items.title: Rondje Buitenland Extra: Arsenal kraakt en verbazing over commotie Vinícius
items.summary: Kenner buitenlands voetbal Süleyman Öztürk neemt in Rondje Buitenland Extra de voetbalweek door. De titelstrijd in de Premier League is spannender dan ooit: geeft Arsenal het wéér weg? De meeste aandacht in deze speciale aflevering is echter voor al het Champions League-voetbal. Het incident met Vinícius, de prestaties van Teun Koopmeiners en Noa Lang én het succes van Bodø/Glimt komen uitgebreid ter sprake.

items.title: Netball Super League's top three will be tough to topple, says Neville
items.summary: Sky Sports netball pundit Tracey Neville expects to see Loughborough Lightning, London Pulse and Manchester Thunder battling for honours once again in the upcoming Netball Super League season.

items.title: BBC to show Wrexham v Chelsea & Wolves v Liverpool in FA Cup
items.summary: The BBC will televise Wrexham v Chelsea and Wolves v Liverpool in the fifth round of the FA Cup in March.

items.title: Champions League review: Bodø shock again, PSG escape and Mourinho’s dismal comments
items.summary: Racism allegations in Portugal overshadowed another fine result in the Arctic and the holders being pushed by their Ligue 1 rivals Nothing should divert attention away from what happened after Vinícius Júnior’s goal for Real Madrid in their 1-0 victory at Benfica on Tuesday. It would be frivolous to do so. The Brazilian scored one of the finest goals of a career marked by spectacular strikes, but this week’s Champions League action will be remembered for the regrettable flashpoint that followed. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Another nonsense!' - Wolff fumes despite accepting proposed engine change
items.summary: Mercedes boss Toto Wolff says he is "philosophically" opposed to the FIA's proposal of a mid-season rule change over a contentious engine element, but says his team are willing to be "good citizens" by accepting it despite it potentially harming their performance.

items.title: Saka rejects talk of Arsenal wilting but scars of title near-misses run deep
items.summary: Late Wolves leveller means seven dropped points from winning positions in 2026 – and Manchester City are lurking It was left to Bukayo Saka to sum up the mood in Arsenal’s dressing room. “Very flat,” admitted the England forward after watching his side surrender a 2-0 lead at Wolves on Wednesday night. A couple of hours earlier, Saka’s first goal in 15 games in all competitions – his longest drought since breaking into the first team as a fresh-faced teenager in 2018 – looked to have set up an easy victory over the Premier League’s bottom side to restore Arsenal’s seven-point cushion over Manchester City. Made captain for the night by Mikel Arteta in the continued absence of Martin Ødegaard, Saka celebrated his rare headed goal by mimicking signing the lucrative contract to 2030, worth more than £300,000 a week, that was announced by Arsenal on Thursday. But his broad smile had turned to a frown by the time he faced the television cameras in the tunnel at Molineux. Continue reading...
